content.title: Agent Workstation
content.subtitle: One install. One CLI. One DB. All agent capabilities. Any coding agent.

sections.heading: The Problem
sections.content: Today's AI coding agent tooling is fragmented: 6+ repos, 3 databases, 4 CLIs, kiro-only. Developers juggle krew-cli for orchestration, kong for runtime, issue-tracker for issues, knowledge-graph for search — each with its own install, DB, and integration quirks. Agent Workstation unifies everything into a single coherent platform that works with ANY coding agent.

sections.heading: How It Helps Developers
sections.content: Agent Workstation gives developers a complete AI-native development environment from a single CLI. Instead of learning 4+ tools and managing multiple databases, you get one unified interface that handles spawning agents, enforcing quality pipelines, tracking issues, learning from failures, and coordinating multi-agent work — all from your terminal.

sections.heading: Developer Workflow: From Task to Done

items.title: 1. aw init
items.description: Scaffold project with SDD methodology, constitution, roles, and quality gates. One command sets up the full AI-native environment.

items.title: 2. aw spawn planner "design feature X"
items.description: Planner agent writes spec with EARS acceptance criteria. Information barrier: coders never see the spec — tests ARE the spec.

items.title: 3. Pipeline: test → sprint → review
items.description: Test-manager generates failing tests (RED gate). Sprint-manager dispatches coders in parallel waves. Reviewer does adversarial review.

items.title: 4. Quality gates enforce standards
items.description: Machine-checkable gates at each transition. No agent claim without evidence. Pipeline blocks until verification command passes.

items.title: 5. aw heuristic — learn from failures
items.description: Post-session reflection extracts lessons. Next session's agents get relevant heuristics in their briefings. System gets smarter over time.

left.title: With Agent Workstation
items: One CLI: aw spawn, aw plan, aw issue
Any coding agent (kiro, aider, claude-code, codex)

sections.heading: Agent-Agnostic Protocol
sections.content: Any coding agent that can read a markdown briefing and write a markdown result can participate. Native runtime (kong loop) for maximum integration, or adapters for external agents. Aider gets --message with the task. Claude-code gets --print. The pipeline doesn't care what's inside the pane — it only cares about the result file.

rows: Coders never see spec, tests ARE the spec
Replace consumed tool outputs with summaries
Extract patterns from failures, inject into future briefings
No merge conflicts between parallel agents
Smart retry vs escalate vs stop
Start tasks as deps complete, don't wait for full wave

content.text: Research validated: our architecture is ahead of the market on orchestration, pipeline enforcement, and multi-agent coordination. The gaps (observation masking, multi-provider, state persistence) are bounded additions, not redesigns.

sections.heading: For Developers: What Changes
sections.content: Install agent-workstation globally. Run aw init in your project. You get: a coding agent that follows SDD methodology, enforces quality gates, learns from its mistakes, recovers from crashes, works with multiple LLM providers, and coordinates multi-agent plans — all from one aw command. No more juggling 4 CLIs. No more lost state. No more starting every session from scratch.

items.title: Phase 1: Core Foundation
items.description: Package structure, agent protocol types, session client, unified DB schema, plugin interface.

items.title: Phase 2: Runtime
items.description: Kong agent loop → @agent-workstation/runtime. Observation masking, multi-provider, checkpoints.

items.title: Phase 3: Orchestration
items.description: Plan system, heuristics, DAG dispatch, error classification, circuit breaker.

items.title: Phase 4: Workflow & Quality
items.description: Pipeline FSM, configurable gates, SDD/TRIO enforcement, review tiers.

items.title: Phase 5: Knowledge & Plugins
items.description: Issues, knowledge graph, browser, devops, remote sync.

items.title: Phase 6: Agent Adapters
items.description: Native (kong), kiro, aider, claude-code, generic file-based.
